title: Composition & framing (cinematography course essentials)
sources_note: Distilled from StudioBinder composition guides, film-school framing 101, and DP masterclass language (e.g. intentional center vs thirds).

principles.use: Default balanced placement; eyes near upper third for CU/MCU
principles.break_when: Power, ritual, or graphic design wants center punch

principles.use: Authority, confrontation, symmetry, Wes-Anderson-style formalism, isolation
Keep intentional: too much headroom can feel small/lost; too little feels cramped
Leave space in gaze or travel direction; reverse for trapped/blocked feeling
principles.break_when: Character is cornered or rejected by frame edge

principles.use: Doorways, shoulders, glass, foliage add depth and voyeurism
principles.break_when: Clean graphic look or product-clarity INSERT

principles.use: Sell cinematic space; combine with slow lateral for parallax
principles.break_when: Flat graphic poster look is intentional

principles.use: Architecture and roads pull eye to subject or reveal

ai_prompt_hints: Describe frame geography in visible facts (left third, centered, over shoulder FG), not abstract theory names alone
For people: protect eyes and readable face unless story hides them
For INSERT: fill frame with object material detail; no invented facial micro-acting
